behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue writer), not just one pure function. No real OS/network dependency is required, no AI dependency, and no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure; in-process wiring can expose this defect.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with a real request object containing a tampered body and mismatched `X-Signature-256`; assert HTTP 401 and assert the queue write path is not invoked and no record is persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (handler, persistence, response path). It needs real wiring with a persistent backend, but not necessarily real network/subprocess. No AI/ML and no agentic sequence. Rejected System because behavior text does not require external API/OS boundary; ordering can be proven in-process with real queue storage.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue persistence implementation (e.g., sqlite/file-backed adapter), invoke the webhook handler, and assert the queue record commit/readback occurs before the HTTP 200 is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears in orchestration between handler, enqueue path, and async downstream worker completion. Real external infra is not strictly required; controlled in-process async dependencies can expose it. No AI dependency, no multi-step agentic flow. Rejected System because text requires non-blocking behavior, not real network/subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a downstream processor that intentionally blocks (unresolved promise / long delay), send a valid webhook, and assert HTTP response is returned promptly while downstream completion remains pending.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is an externally visible latency SLA under concurrency and wall-clock timing; real runtime scheduling and HTTP serving behavior matter. Mocked/in-process shortcuts can hide queueing/backpressure issues that appear in deployed conditions. No AI/ML and no agentic workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service in a real process listening on a real port, create slow concurrent downstream load from earlier events, send webhook requests via real HTTP client, and verify end-to-end response latency stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on state handoff across handler + idempotency check + persistent queue, beyond a single function. Real OS/network is not required; real persistence wiring is. No AI/ML and no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text does not require external boundary beyond local service components.
behaviors.test_description: Using the real persistence-backed dedup path, submit the same event ID twice and assert only one queue record exists (read back persisted state/count).

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This verifies contract mapping from queue-capacity signal to HTTP error response across modules. Can be validated with real handler+queue integration without real external infra. No AI/ML and no agentic sequencing. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network failure modes, only internal condition-to-response mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the queue to full state in an integration setup, send a valid webhook, and assert response is HTTP 503 with a valid `Retry-After` header and no enqueue success.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets real queue implementation behavior with persistence, which unit mocks can hide. It is still local component integration (storage + queue logic), not necessarily OS/network/subprocess-level system behavior. No AI/ML and no agentic chain. Rejected System because behavior text is about internal persistent queue semantics, not external infrastructure contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with capacity N using the real persistence backend, enqueue N events successfully, attempt N+1, assert rejection, and read back persisted state to confirm stored count does not exceed N.
